root_cause_analysis.symptom: Prior cycle 20260519-175339 self-identified 10 shortcomings during /close debate; 6 were classified SYSTEMIC infra defects, 3 NEED-TO-FIX behavioral disciplines, 1 ACCEPTABLE-DEBT.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Cumulative infra debt: (a) tool-policy contract inconsistency with agent.md codex_required mandates (item 7 — recurs every cycle); (b) pattern-match grant mechanism is trivially spoofable + bypassable (item 2); (c) push-grant Chain-B short-circuit relies on orchestrator chaining semantics (item 1); (d) commit-grant TTL structurally too short for subagent latency (item 4); (e) /commit Step 7 silent skip is fail-open data contract (item 5); (f) QA test harness has no trap-on-exit (item 10); (g) discipline reminders missing in close.md/dev.md/qa.md/commit.md (items 3, 6, 9).
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A (retrospective items span multiple commits and infra design decisions; no single commit caused all 10)
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Each item has its own genealogy; retrospective JSON items[].final_rationale fields document each one. Tool-policy gaps were original 2026-05-09 omissions; grant-pattern matching was original 2026-04 design; push-analyst TTL was 60s and bumped to 120s in a prior cycle but is still too tight.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Recurrence — every cycle hits at least some of these gaps. This BA session hit item 7 live during codex consult (codex tee blocked).
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Items accumulated across multiple cycles; the prior cycle 20260519-175339 was the first to formally enumerate them in a structured retrospective. The retrospective was produced 2026-05-20T05:05:00Z; this BA spec follows at 2026-05-20T05:30:00Z.

The trap-cleanup pattern moved to agents/qa.md subsection per iter-3 AC10 V2. 2 OBSERVATION_ONLY logged: CF2-01 (AC1 awk comment-strip confirmation), CF2-10 (pretool-write-guard.sh overwrite-block is separate layer AC7 cannot fix).

findings.issue: Draft lists retrospective items but does not map them to AC1.1..AC2.3.
findings.proposed_fix: Add 9-row traceability table.
findings.rationale: Requirements Decomposition table already maps R1..R9 to AC1..AC10 (with AC8 acceptable-debt OOS); user requirement AC1.1..AC2.3 correspond 1:1 via the retrospective item numbers — AC1.1=item7=AC7, AC1.2=item2=AC2, AC1.3=item1=AC1, AC1.4=item5=AC5, AC1.5=item4=AC4, AC1.6=item10=AC10, AC2.1=item6=AC6, AC2.2=item3=AC3, AC2.3=item9=AC9.

findings.issue: Bare Skill in inspector allowed_tools is over-broad; may permit human-only skill invocation.
findings.proposed_fix: Prefer exact skill-name allowlisting or pair with Skill(name:<human-only>) denies.
findings.rationale: Per global CLAUDE.md Lesson #15, human-only skill isolation is a settings.json Skill(name:*) deny mechanism — DIFFERENT layer from tool-policy.v1.json. The retrospective's concrete_remediation for item 7 specifies bare Skill in tool-policy; settings.json hardening is OOS for this retrospective cycle. Recorded for future cycle.

findings.issue: Replacing only match_grant_for_bash_command is incomplete if grant creation still emits substring/regex fields.
findings.proposed_fix: Define minimal allowed_operations[] schema; update grant writer; deny-by-default on missing/malformed; remove legacy substring auth.
findings.rationale: Files-to-Modify list item #3 already covers hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh (the grant writer). BA spec mandates 'predicate never substring-matches against the command line' which removes legacy authorization. Manifest schema is in Technical Hints.

findings.issue: 'sentinel-file gate' underspecified; a stale/advisory sentinel would not make validate+push atomic.
findings.proposed_fix: Specify sentinel invariants: atomically created on validate, bound to expected HEAD/tree/token, consumed only by same push flow, cleaned on all exits, stale → deny.
findings.rationale: Added Sentinel invariants sub-block under AC1: atomic write-temp+rename, REQUEST_ID binding, one push = one sentinel, single-use consume, stale-mtime deny, trap-cleanup invariant.

findings.issue: 3-stage lookup risks greedy-grep widening via broad glob/content search.
findings.proposed_fix: Use context.spec_path first; close-report fallback exact-path only; final glob filenames/mtime only + exact task-id predicate; no content scan.
findings.rationale: Tightened AC5 stage-3 to metadata-only matching with single literal ${TASK_ID} token grep; explicit prohibition on free-form content scan.

findings.issue: Moving trap-cleanup to qa.md + ba.md can become broad template rewrite.
findings.proposed_fix: Insert only the narrow trap-cleanup contract; explicit DO NOT clauses (no frozen-spec edit, no cleanup outside own artifacts, no hook bypass).
findings.rationale: Added 4 verbatim DO NOT clauses to AC10. AC10 also restructured to explicitly NOT edit frozen spec-20260520-044700.md per user binding directive (reconciled retrospective concrete_remediation 'or wherever the validation recipes live' to agent-template level).

findings.issue: 'codify in_progress-before-Agent' is too vague if left as prose.
findings.proposed_fix: Add normative text: before every Agent dispatch in /dev or /close, matching Todo item MUST already be in_progress; otherwise do not dispatch.
findings.rationale: The Item 3 implementation guidance in Technical Hints already provides exact verbatim normative wording for both files. No additional change needed.

findings.issue: 'Forward-fix only' could be misread as eliminating reversal citations because no git revert occurs.
findings.proposed_fix: Separate the rules: no revert/reset (independently); when a forward fix intentionally reverses prior behavior, changelog MUST include 'Reverses <SHA>: <one-line rationale>'.
findings.rationale: Added Codex CF-10 clarification sub-block to AC9 with TWO independent rules: destructive-verb prohibition (pre-existing per Destructive-Action Escalation) + reversal-citation rule (THIS AC). The two are independent.

findings.issue: AC1 awk regex `^[[:space:]]*git push` does NOT match `# git push` — confirm.
findings.rationale: Confirmed: leading whitespace + `g` literal does not match a `#` comment line. Iter-2 AC1 verifier still uses this regex AS PART OF a fuller comment-strip pre-pass + executable-context detection, addressing CF2-02's broader concern.

findings.issue: AC1 still admits superficial pass via non-line-start executable git push (`if git push`, `command git push`, `exec git push`, `env X=1 git push`).
findings.proposed_fix: Comment/fence-strip pre-pass + executable-context detection (regex matching git push after `(^|[[:space:]]|;|&&|\|\||$(|`)`) + terminal-abort-action requirement (`exit [1-9]`, `return [1-9]`, `exec ...`). Unit test mocks git and asserts mock-invocation count is zero on expired/FAIL sentinel.
findings.rationale: AC1 V1 awk now strips comments via sed first, detects git push in any executable form via a broadened regex, and requires a terminal abort action before any such invocation. AC1 V5 mandates a unit test with mocked git and zero-invocation assertion.

findings.issue: AC2 test names allow dummy/skipped tests.
findings.proposed_fix: Require actual `def test_*` definitions via AST; forbid `@skip/@xfail`; run the specific test file; assert allowlist file/key removed for each terminal case.
findings.rationale: AC2 V2 is now a Python AST verifier that rejects @skip/@xfail-decorated tests. AC2 V3 runs `pytest -k '...' --no-header -q` requiring zero skips/xfails. Per-test post-condition asserts grant file under /tmp/claude-grants/ is unlinked.

findings.issue: AC3 phrase can match nearby examples/comments/code fences/negating polarity.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract actual Agent-dispatch section, ignore fenced code, require positive imperative.
findings.rationale: AC3 V2 is fence-aware awk that tracks the current `##` section. Phrase MUST appear in a `## Step <N>` or `Agent dispatch` sub-section, not in Appendix/Future/Notes/History/Changelog. Negating polarity (`do not`, `skip`, `ignore`) is rejected on the phrase line. AC3 V3 requires positive-imperative wording within 3 lines after.

findings.issue: AC4 negative-grep enumeration incomplete (`minutes=3`, `seconds=3*60`, `seconds=60+60+60`, `seconds=int("180")`, `seconds=0xb4`, aliased `td(seconds=...)`, dead-code constant + real-code literal).
findings.proposed_fix: Replace negative-grep enumeration with AST check: exactly one module-level `PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180`, grant expiry uses `timedelta(seconds=PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS)`, no other timedelta call on grant-expiry path.
findings.rationale: AC4 V2 is now a Python AST verifier that extracts the Phase 7 ```python``` block from agents/push-analyst.md and asserts: (a) PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS appears exactly once as a module-level assign with literal 180; (b) every timedelta(...) call (incl. aliased imports) takes the named constant as its sole argument. Catches all enumerated drift variants by construction.

findings.issue: AC5 close-report parsing fooled by code fences.
findings.proposed_fix: Parse close report fence-aware; accept exactly one non-fenced, non-indented line matching the regex.
findings.rationale: AC5 Step 7 algorithm text explicitly mandates fence-aware parsing (`read each line outside a fenced code block (skip ranges between ``` and ```)`). V2 verifier asserts this language is present.

findings.issue: AC5 nondeterminism can be reintroduced with different wording (`pick latest`, `prefer newest`, etc.).
findings.proposed_fix: Broaden forbidden-phrase grep + verify exact ordered algorithm text structurally.
findings.rationale: AC5 V1 is a structural awk that asserts 5 algorithm step markers appear in monotonic order within the Step 7 section. AC5 V3 forbids broadened nondeterminism aliases: `dev chooses`, `one of:`, `either.*mtime.*or.*filename`, `pick latest`, `prefer newest`, `select any`, `most recent wins`, `any matching`, `the right one`.

findings.issue: AC6 polarity can be inverted (`not a blocking objection`, `do not fail verdict`).
findings.proposed_fix: Require positive normative wording (`MUST raise a blocking objection`, `verdict MUST be FAIL`), ignore fences.
findings.rationale: AC6 V4 grep is now positive-normative phrases (`MUST raise a blocking objection`, `verdict MUST be FAIL`, `MUST NOT downgrade to warning`, `is a blocking BA-validation objection`) — not bare nouns. Negated forms cannot match because the positive phrases include MUST/MUST NOT as part of the literal.

findings.issue: AC7 codex-detection patterns miss variants (`Skill(name:codex)`, `/codex`, `$codex`, prose).
findings.proposed_fix: Expand detection patterns; verify policy and any settings deny/allow rules agree.
findings.rationale: AC7 V1 detection regex broadened to `codex_required|Skill\(skill=["']codex["']\)|Skill\(name:codex\)|/codex\b|codex consultation|invoke codex|Skill\(codex\)`. Catches all enumerated variants.

findings.issue: AC7 write-path invariant cannot fix pretool-write-guard.sh overwrite-block on stale existing content.
findings.rationale: Confirmed separate layer. AC7 fixes only allowed_write_path_prefixes; the hook-level overwrite-block is logged as out_of_scope_observation #4 for a follow-on cycle. Two QA iter-1 + BA iter-1 incidents independently demonstrate the separation.

findings.issue: AC9 `grep -B30` proves nearby Phase 6 heading, not section membership.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract Phase 6 section bounded by next heading, ignore fences, require exact phrase there; separately assert affected_files includes agents/changelog-analyst.md.
findings.rationale: AC9 V1 is fence-aware awk that extracts content between `### Phase 6` and `### Phase 7`. AC9 V4 asserts via jq that affected_files contains agents/changelog-analyst.md. AC9 V3 explicitly FAILS the AC if the phrase exists only in commands/commit.md (i.e., reverses the iter-1 ambiguity).

findings.issue: AC10 trap-before-spawn structurally inert if `$PID` expanded too early, in different variable, in fence/comment, or inactive branch.
findings.proposed_fix: Require PID="" pre-init, single-quoted trap or cleanup function using runtime `"${PID}"`, spawn immediately assigning PID=$!, cleanup targeting only `"${PID}"`, no pkill -f.
findings.rationale: Iter-2 applied this PROPOSED_FIX by adding a Python verifier that extracted ```bash``` blocks from docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-044700.md. BA-QA iter-1 OBJ-1 (BLOCKER) ruled the underlying premise — editing the frozen spec to add verification-recipe blocks — violates user requirement line 76. Iter-3 retains the SAME structural-ordering invariant (PID="" → trap with double-quoted runtime "${PID}" + EXIT INT TERM → PID=$! spawn capture, no pkill -f / killall) but applies it in the new agents/qa.md `### Verification harness cleanup contract (MANDATORY)` subsection per iter-3 AC10 V2 awk. CF2-12 is therefore APPLIED IN SPIRIT but the original verifier targeting the frozen file is REJECTED.

findings.issue: AC10 frozen-spec reconciliation is currently a contradiction risk.
findings.proposed_fix: Pick one invariant. Recommended: AC10 explicit as append-only — no edits to existing frozen content, only additive verification-recipe sections; W7 frozen-COMMIT invariant on d988d4a unchanged.
findings.rationale: Iter-2 picked the append-only invariant (V5: existing 73 lines from d988d4a as verbatim prefix of working-tree file). BA-QA iter-1 OBJ-1 (BLOCKER) ruled the append-only invariant ALSO violates user requirement line 76 because the user said 'do not modify' without an append-only carve-out. Iter-3 picks the OTHER invariant explicitly: ZERO diff between working-tree and d988d4a (AC10 V4: `git diff d988d4a -- docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-044700.md` must return 0 lines). The frozen file is byte-identical to d988d4a. CF2-13's contradiction-risk concern is closed by the stricter invariant.

findings.issue: workflow_routing _test_writer_skip_reason can be decorative only.
findings.proposed_fix: Add verifier that dev.md Step 8 reads _test_writer_skip_reason and treats non-empty value as explicit SKIP.
findings.rationale: BA ticket gains a Test-Writer Routing Disposition section with V_TW verifier: (1) jq asserts _test_writer_skip_reason non-empty in context.json; (2) commands/dev.md Step 8 honors the sentinel (`grep -nE '_test_writer_skip_reason|test.writer.*skip|skip.*test.writer' commands/dev.md` returns ≥1 match within Step 8); (3) if Step 8 doesn't yet honor, R3 reminder cycle absorbs the one-line addition.

findings.issue: AC5 still contradicts the item-5 remediation order — iter-3 algorithm at ticket:387-389 does context.spec_path first and STOP, but retrospective JSON line 86 said 'glob before reading context.spec_path'.
findings.proposed_fix: Change AC5 to collect/validate close-report + cycle-window glob evidence before trusting context.spec_path, or compare all discovered sources and fail-closed on mismatch/null context.
findings.rationale: Codex re-opens the algorithm-order design choice. The orchestrator's iter-3 dispatch explicitly enumerated three blockers (OBJ-1 frozen-spec contradiction, OBJ-2 regex drift, OBJ-3 dual-landing) — algorithm order was NOT among them. QA iter-1 OBS-2 (one of the 7 observation_only findings) DID raise this same concern as a secondary concern and explicitly said context-first is 'defensible if context reliable'. The orchestrator's iter-3 dispatch said 'Don't expand scope chasing them' regarding the 7 observation_only findings. Reopening the algorithm-design debate is scope expansion. Surfaced to user as observation for separate cycle.

findings.issue: AC5 V1/V5 verifiers use ^## Step 7 but commands/commit.md uses ### Step 7; V1 step-2 marker won't match the BA-mandated algorithm prose line (2) Continuation spec line; V5 grep is whole-file not section-scoped.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract Step 7 with heading-tolerant bounds ^#{2,3}[[:space:]]+Step 7 through next Step/section heading. V1 step-2 marker must accept algorithm-prose form, regex literal form, and back-compat form. V5 grep operates on the extracted section.
findings.rationale: Real defect in iter-3 surface QA iter-1 OBJ-2 cited. Applied: shared extractor /tmp/step7.md uses ^#{2,3}[[:space:]]+Step 7; V1 step-2 marker accepts three forms (algorithm-prose `(2) Continuation spec`, regex literal, back-compat); V2/V3/V5 operate on /tmp/step7.md. Smoke-tested against synthesized dev output — V1 passes with markers in monotonic order.

findings.issue: Residual active frozen-spec edit/append references remain in context.json: line 14 'continuation-spec', line 319 'modifies ... continuation spec', CF2-12/CF2-13 disposition still 'applied'.
findings.proposed_fix: Rewrite those fields to agent-template-only language; mark CF2-12/CF2-13 as SUPERSEDED/REJECTED by iter-3, not applied.
findings.rationale: Direct continuation of QA iter-1 OBJ-1 (remove ALL frozen-spec edit references). Applied: requirement.what reworded to explicitly state the frozen file is NOT modified; novelty-check rationale updated; iter_2_summary marks CF2-12/CF2-13 as SUPERSEDED by iter-3; findings[] CF2-12 and CF2-13 changed disposition from 'applied' to 'superseded_by_iter3' with iter-3 rationale.

findings.issue: Context still points to stale wrong-cycle AC JSON via acceptance_criteria_path while _test_writer_skip_reason flags the file as stale D+H content.
findings.proposed_fix: Set acceptance_criteria_path to null while _test_writer_skip_reason is active, or regenerate a correct AC JSON mirror.
findings.rationale: One-line consistency fix. acceptance_criteria_path set to null so downstream test-writer/QA reads ACs from ticket prose (AC1..AC10), not from the stale D+H file. The stale file itself is R7-blocked write per OBS #4 and is left alone.

findings.issue: AC10 V1 truncates DO-NOT clauses (checks substrings for pkill/killall and frozen-spec); V3 whole-file grep instead of section-bounded check for BOTH AC template + Technical Hints template in agents/ba.md.
findings.proposed_fix: V1 grep FULL clause text including (no pkill -f, no killall) and the full frozen-spec sentence. V3 add section-bounded checks for both agents/ba.md template locations.
findings.rationale: AC10 V verifiers are the iter-3 surface QA iter-1 OBJ-1 named. Applied: V1 now requires FULL clause text (no truncation) including `(no `pkill -f`, no `killall`)` and the complete frozen-spec sentence; V3 replaced with a Python verifier that extracts the agents/ba.md Markdown Spec template region, locates both the ## Acceptance Criteria and ## Technical Hints sub-sections within it, and asserts the cross-reference appears in BOTH.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: ITER-1: 10 codex findings reviewed; 7 actionable PROPOSED_FIXes applied to AC1 (sentinel invariants CF-06), AC5 (greedy-grep prohibition CF-01/CF-07), AC9 (two-rule clarification CF-10), AC10 (DO-NOT clauses + frozen-spec reconciliation CF-08), Won't Have (frozen-commit invariant CF-02), Files-to-Modify (CF-05 confirmed coverage). 3 observation-only LOGGED (CF-03/CF-04/CF-09). ITER-2: 14 codex findings reviewed; 12 PROPOSED_FIXes applied to AC1/AC2/AC3/AC4/AC5/AC6/AC7/AC9/AC10/workflow_routing as detailed in findings[] above. 2 OBSERVATION_ONLY logged (CF2-01 confirms AC1 awk comment-stripping is safe; CF2-10 confirms pretool-write-guard.sh overwrite-block is separate from AC7's allowed_write_path_prefixes layer). Iter-1 codex output archived at /tmp/codex-ba-redev-output.txt. Iter-2 codex output archived at /tmp/codex-ba-iter2-output.txt.
